feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Not able to extend "My Inbox(F0862)" application for Purchase Requisition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I’m currently working on extending the standard My Inbox app for Purchase Requisition approvals in S/4HANA 2022. The main requirement is to display a list of approvers in the approval detail screen of the app.

I have followed the steps from these SAP Community blogs:
Fiori My Inbox 2.0 – Extend Approve Purchase Order – S/4HANA 1610
Extend My Inbox 2.0 Purchase Requisition S/4 Hana

What I’ve Done:

  • Extended standard CDS views:
    • C_PurRequisitionFs
    • C_PurRequisitionItemFs
    • Added the required fields and defined @ui annotations like UI.LineItem and UI.Identification
  • Verified that the new fields are available in:
    • OData $metadata
    • Annotations preview (/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/catalogservice;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='C_PURREQUISITION_FS_ANNO_MDL',Version='0001')/$value)

Issue:

Although the fields appear correctly in the metadata and annotation files, they are not displayed in the UI of the My Inbox app(Purchase Requisition Approval).

What I’ve Verified:

  • CDS annotations are correctly defined
  • OData metadata is refreshed and active

Question:

Has anyone implemented a similar approver list extension for this scenario successfully?

Any suggestions or shared experiences would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you!

entry.title: The transaction ABUMN doesn"t have the input field -Unit of measurement and has an error message
entry.content: Our business can"t to any partial transfer with the transaction ABUMN, as they always get the standard error message "Enter both quantity and unit of measure". Please see the screenshot in the attachment. The problem is not only in FIP, but also in every test and develpment system. Could you please help how to set the field "unit of measure"? Thanks.

entry.title: Knowledge sharing - SAP Multi-Client setup and experiences using SAC/Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi all you SAP gurus, 

Does anyone know of companies running a SAP muli-client setup combined with SAC and Datashere?

If yes, how did they accomplish the setup on SAS/SAP Datasphere side etc. in relation to spaces,  joint corporate reporting and so on?

Any comments are welcome 🙂

Thanks in advance,

Michael

May the SAP force be with you all ....

entry.title: Missing tab in SOAMANAGER
entry.content:
Hi Experts!

We're currently activating our Inbound for EDI, and while we run the SOAMANAGER, we didn't see the "Application and Scenario Communcation Tab". 

Is there any updates or changes for this tab name in SOAMANAGER? or is there a guide to add this tab via authorization in SU01 to be visible this in SOAMANAGER?

BTW, currently we're using S4 HANA Private Cloud Edition 2020.

Thank you in advance!
-Jaspher

entry.title: SAP Mobile Start via SAP Start Booster – No Apps or Products Showing Post Setup (Foundation Plan)
entry.content:
Hi @sapcommunity  & @sapexperts 

We are currently working on enabling access to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Fiori applications via the SAP Mobile Start app, using the SAP Start booster in SAP BTP. However, we are facing an issue where no content appears on the SAP Start launchpad—even though the setup completes successfully.

Initial Approach – Manual Configuration

Initially, we tried to manually expose S/4HANA content to SAP Build Work Zone (Foundation Plan):

  • Verified that relevant business catalogs were marked as “Available for SAP Build Work Zone / SAP Start.”

  • Ensured that business roles were properly configured and exposed to SAP BTP.

Challenge:
Due to limitations of the Foundation Plan, we were unable to access the Content Manager or assign roles in the Site Directory, which prevented us from proceeding further with manual setup.

References Followed:
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/Setting-Up-SAP-Build-Work-Zone-standard-edition-and-SAP-Mobile-Start-with-SAP-S-4HANA/importing-content-to-sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition-2-2

Alternative Approach – SAP Start Booster

To overcome these constraints, we executed the “Set Up SAP Start” Booster from SAP BTP. The booster ran successfully and completed the following:

  • ✅ Created the BTP subaccount, assigned entitlements, and configured trust.

  • ✅ Subscribed to SAP Build Work Zone – Foundation Plan.

  • ✅ Configured destinations to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.

  • ✅ Created the default SAP Start site.

  • ✅ Set up SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS and IPS).

  • ✅ Integrated the S/4HANA Cloud system automatically.

As a result, the SAP Start site is now accessible via both desktop and the SAP Mobile Start app.

References Followed:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/setting-up-sap-start-with-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13573816

Current Issue

Despite the successful booster execution:

  • ⚠️ SAP Start site shows the message:
    “Currently, there aren’t any products for you. Please check back again later.”

  • ⚠️ No Fiori apps or S/4HANA content is visible in the launchpad.

Troubleshooting Performed

We have already validated the following:

  • 🔹 Trust configuration between BTP and IAS is correctly set.

  • 🔹 Users are provisioned and mapped properly via IPS.

  • 🔹 Exposed business catalogs and roles in S/4HANA.

  • 🔹 Booster completed without any reported errors.

  • 🔹 Attempted to recheck destinations and Channel Manager (though limited under Foundation Plan).

  • 🔹 Tried accessing SAP Start from both desktop and mobile, same result.

Request for Help / Clarifications

We’d appreciate your support with the following:

  1. Is there any missing step required to make the apps visible after the booster setup?

  2. Does the Foundation Plan impose any limitation that restricts content visibility in SAP Start?

  3. Would upgrading to SAP Build Work Zone – Standard Plan be required for full SAP Start functionality?

  4. Can anyone who successfully implemented SAP Mobile Start via Foundation Plan share insights?

Any pointers or confirmations from the SAP team or community experts would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!
Nithesh T S

entry.title: Update business partner in S/4 using cl_md_bp_maintain
entry.content:
I"m trying to change a business partner"s address data using the cl_md_bp_maintain class. The email address and phone number are updated, but the old numbers are still there. Only the default validity period is entered for the new number. I set the current_state parameter for the numbers to true, but the old numbers remain.

How do I get rid of the older entries?

entry.title: Vendor blocked in S4 but External procurement Rel. is still active in PP/DS despite CIF PIR transfer
entry.content: I got post processing records when Purchase req was being sent from PPDS to s4 , error statement said that vendor is blocked in S4, Hence I did a PIR transfer through CIF Integration model. PIR was transfered but seems like vendor details are not transfered Hence the External Procurement relationship was still active. I had to manually delete it so that no more Purchase req will be created using that. My question is , is there any means to update vender related changes to PPDS in s4 hana , which i can schedule a batch job for or Do I have to manually delete External proc relationships in PPDS whenever someone blocks vendor in S4.

entry.title: Error in outbound IDoc: "EDI: Field ARCKEY in control record with value"
entry.content:
Hi!

Our team is currently trying to establish connection between BTP and S4HANA via IDoc adapter. We already made the inbound connection work. but encountered an error during the outbound flow. We received the following error:

"EDI: Field ARCKEY in control record with value 'urn:sap.com:msgid=<MESSAGE ID>'". The message ID part is a 27-character alphanumeric code.

Is there a way to remove this field? Or a way for this field to automatically not populate any value? I tried manually removing the value via WE19 and it worked, so I figured removing this field is the only way. We cannot find a KB that could solve this issue. 

Thank you very much!

entry.title: How to create communication arrangement
entry.content:
We want to allow a non-SAP cloud application called Quickbase access to our S/4hana Private cloud system. Quickbase has given us a document titled "Set Up Authentication for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions" to follow but this seems to apply to the Public cloud edition only. Could you provide guidance as to how we can create the same extension from our S/4Hana system? Is there a similar guide to follow that shows us how we can expose our API externally to quickbase?

SAP Note 3104502 said you can facilitate communication with OData services by exposing OData ICF services using standard on-premise processes. However, I don't know how to go about this. I need the OData services exposed externally.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How to stop Sending RFQ by Email to Vendor Automatically
entry.content:
Hi everyone! Can someone please tell me where in SAP S4 HANA could i stop the sending of aoutomatic RFQ emils to venderos.

Heres the thing, I dont need vendors to know about the purchase process, because we already know the prices and venders politics, and so those emails were not being sent, but some how now are, and I need to stop at least the process of sending RFQ.

entry.title: End-to-End Maintenance Execution Using SAP Fiori Apps in SAP S/4HANA – A Phase-Wise Approach
entry.content:
Introduction:
In modern asset-intensive industries, the reliability and availability of technical assets directly impact business performance. SAP Plant Maintenance (SAP PM) in S/4HANA, integrated with SAP Fiori, transforms the traditional maintenance execution process into a user-centric, phase-wise digital journey.

This blog outlines the End-to-End Maintenance Execution Cycle using standard SAP Fiori apps, structured by maintenance phases – from initiation to completion.

Maintenance Execution Phases & SAP Fiori Apps:

Below is a breakdown of the 9 maintenance phases, each mapped with relevant SAP Fiori applications used in SAP S/4HANA:

1. Initiation Phase – Raise Maintenance Request

  • App:

    • Create Maintenance Request (F1511A)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1511A')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F1511A

    • My Maintenance Requests (F4513)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4513')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F4513

  • Business Role: Technician / Operator

  • Purpose: Report asset breakdown, malfunction, or maintenance need.

2. Screening Phase – Request Evaluation

  • App:

    • Screen Maintenance Request (F4072)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4072')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F4072 

  • Business Role: Maintenance Supervisor

  • Purpose: Validate, prioritize, and convert valid requests into maintenance notifications.

3. Planning Phase – Notification Planning

  • App:

    • Find Maintenance Notification (F2071)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2071')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F2071

  • Business Role: Maintenance Planner

  • Purpose: Plan operations, materials, required components, work centers, and document scope.

4. Approval Phase – Workflow Approval

  • App:

    • My Inbox (F0862)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F0862')/S26OP

      Fiori ID: F0862

       

       

  • Business Role: Department Manager / Approver

  • Purpose: Approve maintenance plans based on cost, scope, and operational needs.

5. Preparation Phase – Capacity Planning

  • Apps:

    • Manage Maintenance Planning Buckets (F3888)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F3888')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F3888

    • Manage Maintenance Buckets (F4073)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4073')/S30PCE 

      Fiori ID: F4073

       

  • Business Role: Maintenance Planner

  • Purpose: Review capacity, allocate resources into planning buckets.

6. Scheduling Phase – Order Scheduling

  • App:

    • Find Maintenance Order (F2175)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2175')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F2175

       

  • Business Role: Scheduler

  • Purpose: Schedule maintenance orders against available capacity and resource windows.

7. Execution Phase – Perform Maintenance

  • App:

    • Perform Maintenance Jobs (F5104A)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F5104A')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F5104A

       

  • Business Role: Maintenance Technician

  • Purpose: Execute job cards, record time confirmations, consumption, and failure causes.

8. Post-Execution Phase – Technical Completion

  • App:

    • Find Maintenance Order (Set to TECO)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2175')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F2175

       

  • Business Role: Maintenance Supervisor

  • Purpose: Confirm job completion, close technical operations.

9. Completion Phase – Business Closure

  • App:

    • Find Maintenance Order (Set to CLSD)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2175')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F2175

       

  • Business Role: Maintenance Planner / Controller

  • Purpose: Final financial settlement, order closure, cost analysis.


Business Benefits of Fiori-Based Maintenance Phases:

  • Role-based access & intuitive UI

  • Improved maintenance planning & execution accuracy

  • Faster approvals via integrated workflow

  • Real-time visibility into asset status

  • Seamless collaboration between maintenance teams

Conclusion:
SAP S/4HANA with Fiori delivers an intelligent, mobile-ready, and user-friendly maintenance experience. By aligning the maintenance lifecycle with structured Fiori apps, organizations can reduce asset downtime, streamline approvals, and achieve operational excellence.

entry.title: API_EQUIPMENT - How to Update the custom fields in API using PATCH Operation
entry.content:
As per business requirement, I have added Equipment  field User status ( standard field ) in  Standard API_EQUIPMENT  using CDS extended option.

GET Method : It is fetching the Equipment User status value successfully using Get method via Postman.

PATCH Method:  While Updating the Equipment user status is  getting failed using  Patch Method via Postman.

Kindly suggest me how to update the newly added customized fields value using PATCH Method for API_EQUIPMENT.

 

 

 

Regards,

 

S Surya

entry.title: Missing MRP Type error while processing the IDoc for the MATMAS07 message type in EWM
entry.content:
Hi colleagues, 

 

Business Case: We have the Reduced Message Type /BYGL/EWMMATMAS. In Segment E1MARCM, the MRP view fields are deselected as we don’t need in EWM or to keep per SAP’s recommended fields in this segment. (Refer the screenshots for the segment fields at the last in this document).

Even after reducing the MRP fields, still the MRP Type Missing error is coming.

 

Thanks a lot. 

 

Best regards,,

Edward

entry.title: Question regarding Reactive Maintenance (4HH_CA) Minor Work
entry.content:
Hi, this is a question regarding the EAM Minor Work Process.  We have given the authorization for the Maintenance Planner and the Maintenance Supervisor to create minor work maintenance request (Business Catalog SAP_EAM_BC_EXCP_MREQ_MNG_PC restriction type Notification Processing Context IECP_MN.

In the SAP test script for Reactive Maintenance (4HH_CA) section 5.3.1 Minor Work - Create Maintenance Request. The Maintenance Planner creates the minor work maintenance request and once created it is available in the Accepted tab in My Maintenance Request.

In the SAP test script for Reactive Maintenance (4HH_CA) section 5.3.2 Minor Work - Record Malfunction Details. The Maintenance Technician checks the accepted maintenance request and records the malfunction data for the maintenance request created by the planner.  The issue we have is that since the maintenance request for minor work was created by the Maintenance Planner, this request cannot be displayed to the Maintenance Technician.  Our presumption is that you can only see your own maintenance requests in My Maintenance Request.  Can you please confirm that there is an error in the SAP script, or if there is another way for the Maintenance Technician to Record Malfunction details and complete notification for minor work if the maintenance request was completed by the planner or the supervisor role.  Thank you

entry.title: CAT2 variant not displaying for users
entry.content: A variant was created in CAT2 to include a new column "Project Description". Variant was created and saved as "Project Description" in administrator but the users are not able to see it.  SAP Notes 2066612 & 2442592 have already been referenced and provide no solution.

entry.title: Multiple division assign in single shipping data plant
entry.content: I have two Division 40 and 50, i have assign 40 Division in Shipping plant for STO and now i am creating STPO for 50 Division Material, i have found 40 division in Shipping tab in PO but i want it will be 50 because in Material code it is 50, i can not change shipping configuration so kindly suggest any Badi or enhancement

entry.title: Issue with deleted Purchase Requisition still generating MRP demand (Fiori App F4795)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am facing a situation related to purchase requisitions created by MRP and deleted via the Fiori App F4795 — Hand Over Purchase Requisitions (F4795).

Scenario:

A purchase requisition generated by MRP for the parent item was deleted via the Fiori App F4795 Hand Over Purchase Requisitions (Table EBAN, field LOEKZ = X).

However, the MRP still shows a Subassembly Requirement (BB) for the child item, with the same quantity and date as the deleted requisition.

The parent component (semi-finished product) still has a reservation that was not cleared (Table RESB, field KZEAR ≠ X).

  • Has anyone faced similar behavior, where a deleted requisition still generates MRP demand due to an open Subassembly Requirement or an uncleared reservation?
  • Is there any official solution or correction note for this?
  • What is the best practice to ensure that deleted requisitions do not continue generating demand in MRP when using F4795?

I have read the article by @Caetano about this app and would appreciate any help with this difficulty.

Thank you very much in advance for your support!

entry.title: PO custom delivery address not populating for limit line items
entry.content: We have a requirement to show custom delivery address corresponding to limit item of adobeform. However, when we added address using custom fields of  extension point MM_PUR_S4_PO_MODIFY_ITEM, the same is not getting in node of limit item but only purchase order item node. We cannot extend datasource as well.

entry.title: PIR price condition update when contract is created via BAPI
entry.content:
Please suggest on how we can update PIR price and conditions without skipping conditions logic through standard approach or if we can have any other FM/ BAPI available to perform this action.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Alation HANA DB Connector in violation of HANA Runtime License
entry.content:
Alation is a market leader in the Data Catalog space.  They show up in any kind of web research with BW, ECC, and other SAP connectors.  All of those connectors are really the same, an ODBC Connection to the HANA Database.  

As DBA's we know that metadata scans at the HANA level will bear no real fruit.  We should also know, that the Runtime License for S/4HANA does not allow ODBC connections by third parties.  In a nutshell, there appears to be no real support for S/4HANA to be connected to Alation.

If your company is assessing Alation, be very specific in your challenges on whether they work with SAP.  

That said, does anyone know if the Data Catalog in Datasphere will be a connector of any type to Third Party solutions like Collibra, or Alation?
